matrix.coverage_score: Claimed ~47/51 PAR + 5 WIN; credible after attacks ~37-40/51. Dead or unproven as specified: both arena rows (2 safety FATALs), all iteration-heavy PAR claims (no inlining/iteration contract), F9-1 lane SIMD (4-16x off on numeric/columnar — the widest common-scenario hole), read-mostly shared state (no arc-swap-class primitive, pubcell requires Copy), pool-linked traversal (30-60% deref tax vs the claimed 10-20%).
matrix.perf_credibility: High for sealed container/queue/IO kernels — machine-identical to hashbrown/rtrb/liburing shapes, and the radix-sort and affine-SPSC WINs are real. Undercut by: closed classify64 stage algebra instead of general SIMD (refuted), no group-prefetch or snapshot-read path, and every hot-loop number silently assuming intrinsic-grade inlining the spec never grants.
matrix.spec_cost: Claimed 36 rules; hostile recount ~5-8x more — the normative form table (~250-300 op rows with failure and fact-kill columns), a memory-model paragraph per concurrency form, two sub-languages (view, classify64), and 9 ANF pattern cards land at 45-65k tokens, straddling or exceeding its own E3 fail line (>60k).
matrix.safety_cost: Largest TCB on the table: 35-55 KLOC of kernels x per-ISA variants, plus two schema LOWERERS that take adversarial writer input and are only verified on shipped instances. aref zero-check deref and postcondition facts are unreviewed fact channels. The five-part verification battery is the best part of the design.
matrix.writability: Go-shaped consumer surface with decision tables — good in principle; but its own source shapes (Option<&V>, entry Occupied/Vacant, iterators) do not typecheck under the kernel it claims to leave unchanged, failure channels are a 3-way soup memorized per op, and knob cliffs (INLINE, spsc-vs-chan) are never tested.
matrix.fatal_findings: 4 FATAL: (1) aref is Copy + get_uniq under shared arena — two live &uniq to one allocation, a miscompile vector via noalias; (2) arena reset-and-reuse has no re-branding mechanism — well-typed UAF + type confusion; (3) iterator freeze / entry API require per-binding loan tracking the checker story explicitly denies; (4) spec undercount ~5-8x, breaching the design's own preregistered fail line.
matrix.verdict: REJECT as-is. Salvage into the winner: the sealed-kernel discipline and five-part TCB verification battery, hidden-epoch reclamation, affine evring buffer loans, frozen per-form perf pins in CI, and the honest 'radix on closed derived keys' WIN.

matrix.design: design:core (MINIMAL SEMANTIC CORE, cells/sealed/erasure, zero trusted container internals)
matrix.coverage_score: Claimed 44 PAR / 1 WIN; credible ~33-36/51. The concurrency custody block is refuted (spsc/mpmc/mutex revert to trust or die), the reclamation GAP + missing shared-immutable ownership kills ~4 read-mostly rows outright (arc-swap config, shared interner, read-scaling tables, seqlock class), mmap fact forgery kills F5-on-mmap, and safe-source sort/codec claims carry 1.2-2x gaps.
matrix.perf_credibility: The sequential cells+sealed+erasure core is the best checked-source parity story anyone produced — a credible route to Vec/SwissTable machine-event parity — but it is unproven, leans on uncontracted inlining/move-ABI/bulk-ops/iteration, cannot discharge loop-carried obligations (no loop invariants in the checker story), and its custody-typed concurrency cannot hold as specified.
matrix.spec_cost: Claimed 50 rules; hostile recount at v0.6 granularity is 79-124 rules (88-138% kernel growth), failing the design's own >30% overrun tripwire — and the overrun is NOT in the severable custody block. Plus 150-250 uncounted op signatures of shadow spec, plus a frozen invariant language that strands the catalog on the first SOTA algorithm shift (Robin Hood inexpressible).
matrix.safety_cost: Smallest claimed TCB (~30 syscall records) but the claim is dishonest: custody clauses are trusted assertions without a memory model, an interference-freedom check, or token instance binding; mmap content-derived facts are forgeable by an external process; an erasure-totality bug is a miscompile. The 'zero trusted internals' headline does not survive.
matrix.writability: Writers never touch cells/sealed (good), but entry choreography under no-reborrow, the absence of any mutate-during-iteration ops, and two colliding aliasing systems (OWN-7 literal indices vs sealed symbolic ranges) make ordinary maintenance loops command-buffer detours a weak writer must discover.
matrix.fatal_findings: 5 FATAL: (1) no shared-immutable-ownership story at all — the read-mostly hole is far wider than the declared GAP; (2) mmap_ro lets externally mutable bytes forge validate-once facts (elided checks over rewritable content); (3) role/writer tokens forgeable across instances — checked-source data race; (4) custody stability unchecked and no memory model — concurrent forms are TCB with extra steps; (5) rule recount 1.6-2.5x over its own kill bound.
matrix.verdict: REJECT as architecture. Harvest: confined region-parameterized borrow-carrying view structs (the single most valuable delta any design produced), copy struct, the closed atomic vocabulary, split_uniq; keep cells/sealed/erasure alive as a preregistered research track for later de-TCBing of individual sequential kernels.

matrix.design: design:hybrid (EVIDENCE-SPLIT HYBRID, three-tier criterion, n=22 + 8 patterns)
matrix.coverage_score: Claimed 44 PAR / 1 WIN / 6 PARTIAL / 0 silent gaps. After attacks: the WIN dies (float reduction fails the design's own law refuter), and ~6 rows are broken as specified (entry RMW under no-reborrow, interning/parallel_for env-borrow shapes, margin on mmap/subslices, group prefetch through the sealed map, snapshot publish at nonzero update rates). Every break has a named, additive repair supplied by the attackers themselves; post-repair credible ~42-45/51 — the best on the table.
matrix.perf_credibility: Highest: every sealed row inherits its exact SOTA mechanism (SwissTable, B-tree, slotmap, Lamport/Vyukov/Chase-Lev, io_uring), the typed-SPSC and radix-sort advantages are genuine Rust-test wins, and the risky claims (codec margin, checked SIMD) are honestly preregistered with fallbacks. Residual risk is concentrated in one place — the unspecified sealed-form/optimizer boundary (inlining, move ABI, iteration protocol) — which is fixable by contract, not redesign.
matrix.spec_cost: Claimed 47 rules; honest ~250-350 effective including ~120-130 op signatures and the borrow-carrying-value rule family it silently needs; source shapes are written in a language whitefoot is not (?, method chains). Comparable to the others once honest; requires the two-tier spec partition and a token-budgeted catalog counted before code.
matrix.safety_cost: TCB honest in kind — ledgered kernels, no writer-suppliable trust, hidden epoch, per-declaration FFI records — but underestimated ~3-5x in size (realistically 40-60 KLOC with the io driver and runtime). DRF theorem, per-form send/share table, trap scope, and ring teardown must be written before the story closes. All 3 safety FATALs are instances of the shared kernel gap plus one brand bug — both repairable.
matrix.writability: Best consumer story: 3-question decision tree, zero memory orderings, declarative schemas, handles/ids as Copy data. Needs the W1 eval it does not preregister: entry/cursor choreography, find-then-insert and INLINE=0 silent cliffs, and real-ANF example mass are the open hazards.
matrix.fatal_findings: 4 FATAL: (1) entry vacancy token survives intervening rehash — stale-slot write through the TCB boundary; (2) load-bearing APIs store borrows in data (env captures, scoped-spawn environments) — untypeable under the frozen kernel; (3) id<'r,T> brands name the region, not the arena — check-free OOB across sibling arenas; (4) form APIs (option<&V>, guards, cursors) untypeable / rule count ~5x understated. All four are cured by the loan judgment + confined borrow-carrying values + generative brands.
matrix.verdict: WINNER as the governing architecture — conditional on the shared kernel fix (loan/freeze judgment + confined borrow-carrying values), generative arena brands, first-class codegen contracts, the v1 shared-snapshot primitive it already ships, and an honest spec recount. Its three-tier criterion is the only falsifiable placement rule any design offered, and all three attackers independently called the architecture right.

matrix.design: design:schema (PARAMETERIZED FORM SCHEMAS, 11 schemas + verified lowering templates)
matrix.coverage_score: Claimed ~46 PAR / 2 WIN; the sort WIN dies to the inductive-fact gap (alg-kit inner loops keep their bounds checks or become templates), the select.strict WIN survives. ~8 rows off-par as specified: multi-node link surgery (LRU/timer/tree at 3-5x C instruction count), arena cross-references (do not exist in the language), argsort/view_eq (plugs cannot capture context), missing retain/drain/bulk-load. Post-repair (ops relocated into templates, state-threading adopted) credible ~41-44/51.
matrix.perf_credibility: Equal to hybrid wherever a template applies — the templates ARE the SOTA shapes, and instantiation-by-substitution preserves them exactly. Weaker at composition seams (multi-handle surgery, cross-form byte pipelines, mmap-borne inputs) until hot paths move inside templates; NEON/movemask-bridge risk on bytescan flagged honestly by the design itself.
matrix.spec_cost: Best abstraction economy on paper — 11 schemas x closed knobs instead of 22-30 forms is the strongest D2 story offered. But payload undercounted 3-4x (op tables, the SIMD intrinsic enumeration, the layout grammar that converges on Kaitai-class or forfeits its PAR rows, enum semantics ≈ 30-60k tokens), and the mandatory state-threading rewrite touches ~70 signatures before an honest recount is even possible.
matrix.safety_cost: Smallest honest TCB IF the verification bet lands: 5 concurrency kernels + allocator + io glue + proof-artifact checker, with all 11 sequential templates machine-verified rather than trusted. Caveats that must be priced: fact sheets must be machine-proved lemmas of I_Σ (else the templates are TCB with extra steps), the parametric proof checker is itself new trusted code, per-template proofs are ~4 person-weeks, and every op addition re-runs a proof — weeks of latency per catalog finding.
matrix.writability: Closed-enum instantiation is the friendliest surface for weak models (misparameterization is a compile error that enumerates the choices; nothing invariant-bearing is writable). Hazards: state-threaded ops + no error-propagation form make multi-field decode and multi-op helpers the corruption hotspot; three concurrent-map spellings and pool-vs-region ambiguity give real wrong-form rates.
matrix.fatal_findings: 2 FATAL (fewest, both the shared kernel gap): (1) resumable access tokens (entry token, btree cursor, shard/mutex guard, rcu pin) untypeable under no-reborrow/no-borrows-in-data — every workaround reopens invalidation UAF; (2) '&uniq self ops as ordinary calls' incoherent — any helper function can perform exactly one op on a container, so the design's own LRU/timer compositions do not typecheck.
matrix.verdict: RUNNER-UP; merge into the winner: parameterized-schema packaging (closed policy enums as data), relocation of composite hot paths INSIDE sealed forms (pool link-edit/splice, table retain/drain/prefetch_key, btree bulk-load), fact-sheets-as-machine-proved-lemmas, the select.strict lowering-guarantee WIN, and the verified-template path kept as a preregistered upgrade for table/pool with a 4-person-week proof-closure tripwire defaulting to sealed-TCB.

recommendation: Adopt design:hybrid's three-tier evidence criterion as the governing architecture — language-primitive only when a capability is reused by >=3 forms with decidable, syntax-directed client obligations; sealed builtin-TCB when par depends on a partial-initialization invariant or a paper-grade concurrency proof (implement-rarely/use-often); taught composition otherwise — because it is the only stance whose FATALs all have named additive repairs, whose post-repair coverage (~42-45/51 credible PAR plus 3 surviving WINs) leads the field, and whose placement rule is itself falsifiable (a checked form missing its band moves to the sealed tier). The decisive cross-cutting fact of this pass: ALL FOUR designs failed on the same kernel gap — the frozen no-reborrow / no-borrows-in-data / borrow-consumed-by-call rules cannot type entry tokens, cursors, guards, iterators, condvar waits, or any multi-op helper — so the recommendation is explicitly CONDITIONAL on one new, honestly priced ownership mechanism as gate #1: confined region-parameterized borrow-carrying values (taken from design:core's confined-view-structs delta) plus a per-binding loan/freeze judgment (a token/cursor/guard minted from container C freezes C until consumed; issue/consume pairs live in the form table). This is new ownership machinery (~10-15 rules) and must be admitted as such; if it cannot be specified decidably, no stance on the table survives and the fallback is state-threaded ops everywhere with a large writability tax. From the losers take: design:schema — parameterized sealed forms with closed policy enums (11-14 spec objects instead of 22-30 forms), relocation of composite hot paths INSIDE sealed forms (pool link-edit/splice/get_disjoint, table retain/drain/prefetch_key/probe_view-with-threaded-ctx, btree bulk-load — this kills the link-surgery, missing-ops, and group-prefetch holes at their root), fact sheets as machine-proved lemmas of the form invariant rather than human-reviewed assertions, the select.strict verified-lowering WIN, and the verified-template program kept as a preregistered upgrade path for table/pool (4-person-week proof-closure tripwire, defaulting to sealed-TCB on failure); design:core — confined borrow-carrying view structs, copy struct (settles the open records question declaratively), the closed atomic vocabulary, split_uniq as a blessed combinator, and cells/sealed/erasure preserved as a research track for later de-TCBing of individual kernels; design:builtin — the five-part TCB verification battery (differential testing, exhaustive small-bound model checking, sanitizer/fuzz soak, hostile pre-ship review, frozen per-form perf pins as CI build breaks), hidden-epoch reclamation with no user-facing pin/retire API, and the honest radix-on-closed-derived-keys sort WIN. Mandatory amendments the attacks force on the winner: (1) ship the shared-snapshot/epoch read primitive (shared<T> publish/snapshot + rcu_table) in v1 — the read-mostly hole was core's widest FATAL and builtin's largest omission, and sharded locks provably plateau 10-20x under Zipf read traffic; (2) ship a portable lane-arithmetic SIMD value core with named intrinsics and one-time dispatch — builtin's classify64-only stance is refuted (4-16x off on numeric/columnar loops); ordinary writers CALL shipped kernels, kernel authorship is project-tier; (3) promote codegen contracts to first-class CI-gated spec: a designated guaranteed-inline hot-op subset, callee-in-place move ABI, an explicit iteration protocol (internal for_each with guaranteed monomorphized inlining as default), 1-load endian access, strict-select never-branch, zero-memset I/O read path; (4) generative per-arena-value brands (sibling-arena confusion rejected at compile time) or, if that exceeds the frontend-simple budget, an always-on elidable checked arena deref with the traversal cost stated honestly; (5) write the missing semantics before any experiment claims anything: trap = process abort (keep EFF-4, drop 'join propagates'), evring/io-ring drain-on-drop teardown protocol, per-form conditional send/share table, minimal publication-only memory model with a paper-grade DRF review, and explicit fact kill columns (facts are boundary-consumed only in v1; any &uniq escape kills all facts on the binding); (6) drop the float/overflow-checked-int parallel-reduction WIN — it fails the language's own law refuter; surviving WINs are radix sort on closed derived keys, typed SPSC endpoints, and strict-select; (7) adopt a two-tier spec partition as an explicit mechanism (kernel ~60-75 honest rules + a token-budgeted normative catalog appendix <=40k tokens) and draft-and-count it BEFORE implementation, since every stance undercounted spec mass 3-8x; (8) ratify named v1 non-goals with an expedited escalation lane rather than silent gaps: concurrent ordered map (LSM memtable), writable MAP_SHARED IPC, inbound FFI callbacks, and user-authored novel lock-free structures.
catalog_skeleton: seq<T, inline=N, growth in {x2,x1p5}> — sealed parameterized form — F1 growable vector, small/inline vector, byte-builder base — (ptr,len,cap) tail-line push, geometric growth with in-place-grow allocator record, reserve exports cap_slack fact, bulk extend/retain/drain/truncate, guaranteed-inline push/index, stride-1 slice views
ring<T> — sealed (shares seq kernel) — F4 single-threaded deque, worklists, sliding windows — pow2 masked head/tail (1-cycle branchless), one-bulk-copy growth, as_slices views (VecDeque-class)

table<K,V, inline=N, hasher in {fold, sip_keyed, identity, crc}> — sealed parameterized form — F2 general + tiny map/set, interning substrate — SwissTable ctrl bytes, 16B SIMD group probe, 7-bit fragments, 87.5% load, tombstone group elision; loan-typed entry token (one hash one probe), probe_view with threaded context, retain/drain, prefetch_key for group-prefetched joins

btree<K,V, key_kind closed> — sealed — F2 ordered maps with range scans — ~256B nodes, fanout 11-16, branchless in-node scan, leaf-chained loan-typed range cursors, bulk-load-from-sorted (BTreeMap/absl class)

pool<T> — sealed — F3 slab/identity substrate (connections, entities, fd tables, node soups) — intrusive freelist, 64-bit-bounded generations with stated wrap contract, no re-zeroing, dense iteration; in-template link-edit/splice/get_disjoint ops so LRU/tree/intrusive/timer cards hit 2-4-store cost

arena<'a> + generative branded id — language primitive + sealed kernel — F3/F6 build-then-drop object graphs, per-request/frame allocation — bump alloc ~1-2 ns, per-arena-VALUE brand for check-free deref (fallback: always-on elidable checked deref), reset via region reincarnation with retained chunks

buffer core: wbuf + margin views — language primitive — F6 zero-init avoidance, F5 codec/cursor substrate — typed initialized extent (zero memset in I/O paths, codegen-pinned), W-slop margin with initialization-at-introduction and no-forging rules, explicit wbuf-to-margin edge; mmap bytes never margined (masked tails)

layout view — schema-instantiated — F5 packets, DB pages, records, mmap formats — declarative field/endian/offset/align schema, validate-once fact, then 1 load (+bswap) per field pinned by asm-diff CI; data-dependent offsets route through cursor facts

cursor — checked library over margin/loan core — F5 varint/TLV decode, multi-stream bit readers, lz_copy — one unaligned 8B load per varint, per-message amortized bounds, 2-4 interleaved bitstreams, overlap-tolerant wild copies (codec parity preregistered with sealed-kernel fallback)

bytescan + hash kernels — checked library over SIMD core — F5 memchr/memmem/utf8/json-stage1/base64 + crc32c/xxh3/siphash — 32B compare + movemask + tzcnt, pshufb nibble tables, clmul carries, 3-stream hw crc; shipped instances writers CALL, authorship is project-tier

alg kit — sealed sort core + checked search/heap/format — F1 sorting, binary search, priority queues, numeric emission — ipnsort/driftsort with radix auto-dispatch on closed derived keys (WIN), select.strict branchless lower_bound, implicit 4-ary heap, itoa/ryu table kernels; lazy-deletion card replaces decrease-key

machine core — language primitive — F9 all five scenarios — width-explicit SIMD value types + closed named-intrinsic set + one-time ISA dispatch, endian 1-mov loads/stores, single-instruction bit ops (pdep/pext with declared fallback), select hint/strict (verified never-branch), prefetch as never-faulting no-op, NT copy/fill flags, align(N)/cachepad

sync vocabulary — sealed TCB — F7 atomics, mutual exclusion — counter (relaxed), publish/snapshot cell, cas-cell, futex-adaptive mutex<T> with loan-typed guard, once<T>, condvar with consume-and-reissue guard; no rwlock, no user-facing ordering menu

conc_queue<T, endpoints in {spsc, mpmc, steal}, cap=2^k> — sealed TCB parameterized — F4/F7 queues and channels — affine endpoints make SPSC zero-RMW typed (WIN), Vyukov one-RMW-per-op MPMC with futex-parked blocking, Chase-Lev proved once behind the par runtime

shared<T> snapshot + rcu_table<K,V> — sealed TCB — F7 read-mostly shared state, concurrent keyed reads — epoch pin ~2 ns, readers write zero shared lines, linear read scaling; reclamation fully hidden; sharded-map card covers mixed write loads

threads + par — sealed TCB — F7 spawn/join, pools, parallel-for/fork-join — scoped spawn typed by lexical regions, blessed split combinator for disjoint chunks, Chase-Lev substrate, ~20-50 ns task overhead; deterministic reduce for wrapping-int monoids only

io family: file / net / evring / filemap / wal / os — sealed TCB — all of F8 — fill_buf borrow-the-buffer loans, buffer-loan affine moves through the completion ring with specified drain-on-drop teardown, io_uring lowering with epoll emulation, O_DIRECT aligned mode, group-commit WAL + atomic replace + fdatasync escapes, posix_spawn/vDSO/CSPRNG facade

extern-C boundary records — TCB trusted-record class — F8 FFI out-calls — bare ABI call (~1-2 ns), toolchain-gated human-approved records with per-arg ownership/aliasing contracts; inbound callbacks deferred

8 taught composition cards (zero new forms) — patterns — LRU/CLOCK cache, interner, CSR graph two-phase, pool-linked trees, intrusive membership links, timer wheel, sharded map, group-prefetch probe + Eytzinger layout — all riding pool/table/seq in-template ops at their measured-parity costs
language_delta_skeleton: Confined borrow-carrying values: region-parameterized affine structs/enums that may hold borrows, stack-confined, never storable in heap data, consumed like unique borrows at calls — types entry tokens, cursors, guards, iterators, fill_buf views, and borrow-carrying env structs for comparators/parallel bodies (~5-6 rules; from design:core)

Loan/freeze judgment: per-binding lexical loan set — a live token/cursor/guard minted from container C freezes C (no & or &uniq ops on C) until the token is consumed or its region ends; issue/consume pairs declared in the form table; statement-scoped minting for single-op calls on uniquely held bindings (~6-8 rules; the shared-fatal fix, gate #1)

Sealed-form mechanism: opaque parameterized form families with one normative op table per form — ownership modes incl. out-own, effect row, failure channel, postcondition-fact column, fact-KILL column, loan column; single failure principle: programmer error traps deterministically, environmental failure returns Result (~4 rules + counted catalog appendix)

Codegen-contract class (new normative class, CI/asm-gated): designated hot ops inline-into-caller, callee-in-place move ABI, endian load = 1 mov (+bswap), select.strict verified never-branch, zero-memset I/O read path, iteration protocol = internal for_each with guaranteed monomorphized inlining (~3 rules + CI infrastructure)

Fact channel: form-table facts feed the existing requires engine, boundary-consumed only in v1 (no intra-function fact dataflow); any &uniq escape kills all facts on the binding; every fact class hostile-reviewed, and machine-proved as a lemma of the form invariant wherever a verified template exists (~3 rules)

Generative arena brands: each arena.new() mints a fresh per-value brand so sibling-arena id confusion is rejected; region-reincarnation reset retaining chunks; fallback if over budget: always-on elidable checked deref (~3-5 rules)
copy struct declaration: explicit declarative Copy opt-in for all-Copy-field records — settles the open records question (~1-2 rules)

Buffer core: wbuf typed init-extent (affine cursor, monotone commit, freeze exposes exactly the written prefix) + margin views with initialization-at-introduction, no forging/narrowing, explicit wbuf-to-margin edge; mmap bytes never margined (~5-6 rules)

Layout schema declarations + validated(L) fact: constant-offset fields v1 with per-field endian/align, one-time validation, single-load access rule; data-dependent offsets route through cursor (~4-5 rules)

Machine core: SIMD value types + closed op/intrinsic enumeration + one-time ISA dispatch declaration; endian load/store forms; single-instruction bit-op table with declared pre-BMI fallback; select hint/strict; prefetch exempt from validity obligations; NT flags; align(N)/cachepad (~12-15 rules — the largest block, and the price of closing F5/F9)

Concurrency core: scoped spawn/join on lexical regions; per-form CONDITIONAL send/share capability table (mutex<T> shareable iff T sendable, guards never sendable, region-branded tokens sendable only under join-enclosing regions); affine endpoint tokens; minimal publication-only memory model + syncs effect label; DRF-by-construction theorem stated for paper-grade review; trap = process abort ruling (~8-10 rules)
io and blocks effect-row labels (~1-2 rules)
Disjoint-split blessed combinator: for_chunks/split_at disjointness proved once as a template fact, not a general arithmetic judgment (~1-2 rules)
extern-C toolchain-gated trusted records with per-argument ownership/aliasing/lifetime contract vocabulary (~3-4 rules)
Two-tier spec partition meta-rule: kernel rules + normative catalog appendix both counted against the D2 token budget, with a one-page op-addition/versioning procedure (~1-2 meta-rules)
Honest total: ~60-75 kernel rules on the 90-rule base plus a <=40k-token catalog appendix — declared up front, measured in M2, and treated as the D2 budget rather than hidden as documentation
open_questions: Can the loan/freeze judgment be specified in <=15 decidable, syntax-directed rules (per-binding lexical loan sets, no fixpoints) that simultaneously accept push;push, entry-then-fill, cursor loops, and guard/condvar reissue while rejecting iterate-while-mutate and token-across-rehash? This is the existential question — no stance survives a NO.

What is the honest token count of the full normative surface (kernel deltas + ~120 op signatures with failure/fact-kill columns + 8 cards in real ANF) — and does it fit a <=40k-token catalog budget against D2, or must the catalog shrink before any code is written?

Generative per-arena-value brands vs always-on elidable checked deref: which fits the frontend-simple budget, and what does the fallback cost on L1-resident graph/AST traversal vs the C pointer baseline (claimed within 10-20%)?

Iteration protocol: internal for_each with guaranteed monomorphized inlining vs loan-typed external cursors — which one both reaches hashbrown/slotmap iteration parity (asm-verified) and survives a mid-tier W1 trial?

Effect-row treatment of writer-supplied behavior (sort comparators, probe_view eq, par bodies): fixed pure-row plus an explicitly threaded context argument, or a row variable (effect polymorphism)? Write sort's real signature and check it against the no-inference ruling.

Which sealed kernels get schema-style machine-verified templates vs plain trusted TCB: is the parametric table proof closable in ~4 person-weeks with a re-checkable artifact, and is the proof-artifact checker's own trusted size acceptable? (Tripwire preregistered; default is sealed-TCB.)

Is the publication-only memory model provably sufficient for every sealed facade contract (condvar reissue, ring completions, join, snapshot cells), and who performs the paper-grade DRF-by-construction review the standing fact-channel rule requires?

Trap scope final ruling: with trap = process abort (EFF-4 kept), what replaces failure signaling for scoped workers, and what exactly happens to in-flight evring ops, held locks, and loaned buffers at abort — is drain-on-drop teardown specifiable and testable?

Fact invalidation: are boundary-only facts (no sort-then-search elision within a function) an acceptable performance concession, and is the per-op kill/preserve column complete enough to survive hostile review of the channel?

Catalog evolution: what is the one-page procedure for adding an op or revising a shipped contract (re-proof/re-verification latency, spec version compatibility, invalidation of taught writer context), given the base rate of dozens of op additions per form per decade in Vec/hashbrown history?

Owner ratification of named v1 non-goals with an expedited escalation lane: concurrent ordered map (LSM memtable), writable MAP_SHARED IPC, inbound FFI callbacks, user-authored lock-free structures — and ratification that pool's checked recycling supersedes P2's never-recycle ruling as an explicit re-decision.

mmap honesty: with content-derived facts on externally mutable bytes declared non-elidable, what residual per-access cost does the F5-on-mmap path pay, and are the affected coverage rows demoted to PARTIAL accordingly?
measurement_plan: M1 (paper, ~1 week, kills the architecture): write the loan/freeze + confined-borrow-value typing rules and produce machine-checked derivations for six canonical programs — push;push (accept), entry-then-fill with an interleaved insert (reject), cursor loop with attempted mutation (reject), mutex guard + condvar consume/reissue (accept), two-ring endpoint-token swap (reject), borrow-carrying env in a par body (accept). PASS: all six verdicts with <=15 new rules and no flow analysis beyond per-binding loan sets. FAIL: any wrong verdict or >25 rules — no stance survives; stop and redesign the kernel gap before anything else.

M2 (paper, ~1-2 weeks): author the real normative text for seq + table + conc_queue (full op tables with failure/fact-kill/loan columns) plus the LRU and interner cards in strict v0.6 ANF, count tokens, extrapolate to the full catalog. PASS: extrapolated kernel+catalog <=40k tokens. FAIL: >55k — cut the catalog (merge forms, drop ops) before any implementation; 40-55k triggers reduction, not shipping.

M3 (~2-3 weeks): prototype sealed seq/table behind the op table with the codegen contracts. Locked workloads on one x86-64/AVX2 box: 1M u64 push-then-sum vs Rust 1.81 Vec; 1M-key uniform u64 insert+hit vs hashbrown 0.15+foldhash; entry-token counter loop; map-iterate-sum; retain-style expiry sweep. PASS: push/lookup <=1.10x, asm shows zero residual checks after the reserve fact and a vectorized sum, entry loop verified one-hash-one-probe in asm, iterate <=1.2x hashbrown iter, sweep <=1.5x hashbrown retain. FAIL: any >1.25x, entry re-probes, or per-element call overhead in iteration.

M4 (~1 week, parallel with M3): arena brand decision — attempt generative-brand rules (two-sibling-arena confusion program must be rejected) in <=5 rules; if not achievable, measure the checked-deref fallback on a 1M-node L1-resident tree walk vs a C pointer arena. PASS: generative rules land, or fallback <=1.25x C with requires-elision. FAIL: fallback >1.4x — the arena row is demoted and the build-then-drop coverage claim rewritten.

M5 (~2 weeks): W1 writability trial — mid-tier (sonnet-class) model given ONLY the M2 draft, 20 tasks spanning wordcount (entry idiom), LRU promote (pool link ops), session-expiry sweep (retain), two-thread pipeline (spsc-vs-mpmc knob), operand-list churn (inline knob). PASS: >=70% first-green AND >=80% of green solutions choose the par form/knob (within 1.3x of the optimal spelling). FAIL: <50% first-green or median chosen/optimal cost >2x — redesign diagnostics/decision tables before enlarging the catalog.

M6 (~2-3 weeks): spsc + mutex sealed kernels — exhaustive small-bound model checking (GenMC/loom-style, preregistered bound) + 24h TSAN soak + perf: spsc cross-core round trip in [6,15] ns and >=80M items/s batched-32, mutex uncontended lock+unlock <=20 ns, zero RMW on spsc hot paths verified in asm; the two-ring token-swap program rejected at compile time. FAIL: any model/TSAN violation (kills the kernel verification story, not just the number) or perf >10% outside band.

M7 (~1-2 weeks): shared<T>/rcu_table read-mostly bench — 32-core, 1M entries, Zipf(1.0) keys, 99.9% reads + 1 reload/s, vs arc-swap and papaya baselines and vs sharded mutex<table>. PASS: read scaling >=12x at 16 cores, per-read <=2x single-thread hashbrown cost, publish cost bounded and documented. FAIL: read plateau <=4-core scaling — the read-mostly coverage rows are false and the primitive must be redesigned before v1.

M8 (~2 weeks): checked-source SIMD kernels — memchr written in the vector core: >=0.85x memchr-crate 2.7 GB/s on 16KB and 1MB haystacks, <=2.0x its short-input (<=16B) latency, elision report confirming all main-loop bounds discharged; utf8_validate >=0.70x simdutf on ASCII-heavy and mixed corpora including the NEON movemask bridge. FAIL: <0.70x bulk or undischarged main-loop checks — the affected F5 instances move to sealed kernels and the vector core is re-scoped.

M9 (codec bet, preregistered fallback): LZ4 block decode as checked source over margin/wbuf/lz_copy — >=0.75x LZ4 1.9 GB/s on Silesia, malformed-input fuzzing clean (error, never corrupt, margin bytes never observable), applied to iobuf, mmap (no-margin mode), and mid-buffer subslice inputs separately. FAIL: <0.60x or any fuzz property violation — codecs move to sealed TCB kernels and the coverage row stays PARTIAL, as prewritten.

M10 (~1 week): boundary-semantics fault injection — (a) evring teardown: submit a read on a stalled fd, drop the ring, immediately churn the heap under ASAN on C-compiled kernels; zero kernel completions land in freed memory under the specified drain-on-drop protocol; (b) group-prefetch join probe via table.prefetch_key on a 200M-entry DRAM-resident table: >=2x over the naive serial-probe loop. FAIL on (a) is a T1 break in the io tier; FAIL on (b) demotes the hash-join workload row.
